title: pluchea odorata var. normalis
intro: Pluchea odorata var. normalis, also known as the Indian valerian or sweet postman, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Pluchea odorata var. normalis can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and calmness.
